programme.title: Taboo Photos Murder Trial
programme.desc: Jurors learned more about Jade Janks relationship with Tom Merriman in the years leading up to his death. Janks told jurors that he had no issues with her stepdad and the two had a close relationship. She would often call him Dad. Both defendant and victim in this case was on a number of different prescription medication. This would be an important aspect to Merriman's demise since he died of an apparent overdose of a sleeping drug.

programme.title: Taboo Photos Murder Trial
programme.desc: Blame the victim was the theme of Jade Janks defense as she testified on the stand to jurors.

programme.title: Taboo Photos Murder Trial
programme.desc: Jurors was told of the very moment Jade Janks came across naked pictures of herself on her stepdad Tom Merriman's computer. According to the defendant, she feared Merriman's reaction after she came across nude photos of herself on his computer. So, instead of confronting him, Jade Janks instead sought the services of a security guard the same guy prosecutors claimed she tried to recruit in her murder plot.

programme.title: Taboo Photos Murder Trial
programme.sub-title: In The Eight Days To Murder
programme.desc: Jade Janks was emotional as she told the jury that she saw more than 100 nude photos of herself on her stepdad's computer. She claimed that Tom Merriman even cropped some of the pictures of her and divided them up into different folders based on her different body parts. Eight days after she discovered the nude photos on her stepdad's computer Merriman died. During her long testimony to jurors, Janks' defense team wanted her to narrate the days and hours leading up to Tom Merriman's death.

programme.title: Taboo Photos Murder Trial
programme.desc: Jade Janks had more to say on the stand. During a long direct examination, she told jurors her side of the story of events that led to Tom Merriman's overdose. Was he asleep or was he dead? That's what the defendant recalled when she came across the body of Tom Merriman lying there in her car. Janks tried to convince the jury on her side story on what led to her stepdad's death.

programme.title: Taboo Photos Murder Trial
programme.desc: Jade Janks faced cross-examination on the witness stand. She admitted that she didn't tell any close friends or relatives about finding those nude photos on her stepdad, Tom Merriman's computer. Instead, she reached out to a complete stranger she met on Facebook about the whole ordeal. Janks recalled her emotional and mental state in the eight days of the discovery of the nude photos and the victim's death.

programme.title: Taboo Photos Murder Trial
programme.sub-title: Walk Us Through On What Happened
programme.desc: The lead prosecutor was not done probing Jade Janks on the stand. She was testifying for her own defense. She admitted to the jury that she did cover up stepfather's remains after he died. After going over the timeline of events leading up to Tom Merriman's death, the state had Janks go through and explain her text messages. Jurors listened to the defendant while weighing her credibility.

programme.title: Deadly Parade Crash Trial
programme.desc: The State's main focus at this point in the deadly parade crash trial was on calling the survivors to the stand. In all, six people died when an SUV plowed into the crowd.

programme.title: Deadly Parade Crash Trial
programme.sub-title: Clocking In The Speed Of The SUV
programme.desc: Police reviewed video footage to try to see how fast the driver was going when an SUV plowed into the crowd at the deadly Christmas parade crash.

programme.title: Deadly Parade Crash Trial
programme.desc: Darrell Brooks questioned the police detective on the stand about how the speed analysis was done for the deadly parade crash trial. They used video footage of the parade, which was live-streamed.

programme.title: Deadly Parade Crash Trial
programme.sub-title: Judge Explains The Rules To Brooks
programme.desc: A survivor of the deadly Christmas parade crash testified about the moment the SUV hit her and her son. She said that the car ran over her left foot. They were among the sixty-one people injured that day.

programme.desc: In 1986, a psychotic killer shot and killed a police officer at point-blank range, leading investigators in a wild chase across the Midwest. As his tally of murders and abductions grew, the FBI raced against time to put a stop to the deranged gunman.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Six Black men had been brutally murdered; two had their hearts cut out. The only thing connecting the victims was their ethnicity and the fact that each had been shot with .22 caliber bullets.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A sniper murdered two Black men as they jogged with two white women. The FBI placed their suspect on their Ten Most Wanted list and began an intense manhunt. What followed was the arrest, trial and conviction of a white supremacist serial killer.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A stranger snatched an 8-year-old girl as she played in her Tucson, Arizona neighborhood. Within hours, police began to suspect a drifter who was out on parole for abducting and molesting children.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The body of an unidentified young woman turned up on a highway in a small Pennsylvania town. Agents persisted, eventually identifying the body and finding the killer.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Masked bandits pulled off a daring bank robbery in the middle of the day. Without any evidence, FBI agents had to force their own luck in the four-year manhunt for the so-called "Hollywood" bank robber in Seattle, Washington.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1993, a mother, her 8-year-old son and a live-in nurse were brutally murdered by an assassin. The murder-for-hire plot was meticulously planned and executed.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A young mother and her two daughters were sexually assaulted and brutally murdered in Anchorage.

programme.desc: Remorseless murderer Casey Pigge's main ambition is to be the ultimate serial killer. If unrestrained, he remains a deadly threat.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A look into the case of a sadistic killer who posed as an MI5 agent in order to torture and murder his victim.

programme.desc: William Bonin is thought to have raped, tortured and murdered a minimum of twenty-one boys and young men.

programme.desc: Steven Dean Gordon and Frank Cano murdered at least four sex workers in California between 2012 and 2013.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A profile into the murderer who terrorised the gay community as the 'Trash Bag Killer' and confessed to killing 32 victims.

programme.desc: A master manipulator accused of the murder of his wife eventually caught out by a polygraph test - and his own fiancée.

programme.desc: A father took his children out for food after murdering his wife, then strangled them when they returned home.
